title: Remove Timing Belt From Tipper Assembly
summary: Safely remove the timing belt from the tipper pulleys during timing belt replacement by positioning the A-axis and counterbalance, locking out the tipper, opening side panel access, securing both carriages with M6 socket-head screws, removing belt clamps, releasing belt tension, and removing the belt when only the timing belt is being replaced.
when_to_use: Use this procedure for the documented removal portion of timing belt replacement on the tipper assembly when the timing belt must be removed from the pulleys.
not_for: Do not use for operator execution; the documented task includes lockout/tagout and mechanical disassembly.
Do not use for installation, retensioning, adjustment, or recovery actions beyond belt removal because those details are not provided in this source section.
Do not proceed if the A-axis, counterbalance, or carriages cannot be positioned as described before belt tension is released.

responsibilities: Perform lockout/tagout using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
Open access panels, secure the carriages, remove belt clamps, release belt tension, and remove the timing belt within the documented removal scope.
Stop and escalate if positioning, securing, or fastener identification/removal cannot be completed as documented.

safety_notes: This procedure is not support-safe for operator execution.
Perform lockout/tagout on the tipper before accessing internal components.
Install M6 socket-head screws below both carriages before releasing belt tension to prevent the A-axis and counterbalance from dropping.
Stop if the A-axis, counterbalance, or carriages cannot be positioned as described before belt tension is released.
Stop if the securing screws or belt clamp fasteners cannot be identified or removed as documented.
Do not proceed beyond the documented removal scope when the source does not provide additional recovery or adjustment guidance in this section.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the tipper side panels and timing belt area
Ability to perform Operator Station Lockout/Tagout

access_required: Access to the tipper side panels and timing belt area
Ability to perform Operator Station Lockout/Tagout
system_preconditions: A-axis and counterbalance manually positioned between the threaded holes in the actuator base before belt tension is released.
Two holes adjacent to each rail verified, with the carriages positioned between the holes on the respective rails when preparing to secure them.

visual_references.description: Timing belt maintenance diagram for timing belt location, A-axis positioning, counterbalance support, carriage alignment, and side panel access during removal.
Idler pulley assembly maintenance diagram for identifying the idler pulley assembly and belt tension release area.
Related maintenance diagram with similar A-axis and counterbalance positioning context and M6 securing screw placement below both carriages.
Counterbalance weight diagram for general counterbalance identification context.

steps.title: Gather replacement part, PPE, and tools
steps.purpose: Ensure all documented parts, PPE, and tools are available before beginning the removal procedure.
steps.instruction: Gather the replacement timing belt, safety glasses, gloves, torque wrench, 5-mm hex wrench, 6-mm hex wrench, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, Loctite, and sonic belt tension meter.
steps.expected_result: All documented parts, PPE, and tools are available at the work area.
steps.healthy_condition: The listed replacement part, PPE, and tools are present and ready for use.
steps.failure_condition: Required part, PPE, or tools are missing or unavailable.

actions.label: Verify required items
actions.exact_action: Gather the documented replacement part, PPE, and tools: timing belt, safety glasses, gloves, torque wrench, 5-mm hex wrench, 6-mm hex wrench, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, Loctite, and sonic belt tension meter.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the timing belt figure as the primary visual reference for the component being serviced.

stop_or_escalate_if: Required part, PPE, or tools are not available.

source_evidence.text: Gather the documented replacement part, PPE, and tools: timing belt, safety glasses, gloves, torque wrench, 5-mm hex wrench, 6-mm hex wrench, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, Loctite, and sonic belt tension meter.

Nearby text lists the replacement part as the timing belt and identifies required PPE and tools including safety glasses, gloves, torque wrench, 5-mm hex wrench, 6-mm hex wrench, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, Loctite, and a sonic belt tension meter.

steps.title: Position the A-axis and counterbalance
steps.purpose: Place the moving elements in the documented position so the carriages can be secured before belt tension is released.
steps.instruction: Manually position the A-axis and the counterbalance between the threaded holes in the actuator base. Verify there are two holes adjacent to each rail and that the carriages are positioned between the holes on the respective rails.
steps.expected_result: The A-axis and counterbalance are positioned between the threaded holes, and both carriage positions are verified relative to the rail holes.
steps.healthy_condition: Two holes are visible adjacent to each rail, and each carriage is positioned between the holes on its respective rail.
steps.failure_condition: The A-axis, counterbalance, or carriages cannot be positioned or verified as described.

actions.label: Position A-axis and counterbalance
actions.exact_action: Manually position the A-axis and the counterbalance between the threaded holes in the actuator base.

actions.label: Verify carriage alignment
actions.exact_action: Verify there are two holes adjacent to each rail and that the carriages are positioned between the holes on the respective rails.

stop_or_escalate_if: The A-axis, counterbalance, or carriages cannot be positioned as described before belt tension is released.

steps.title: Lock out and tag out the tipper
steps.purpose: Place the equipment in a safe state before opening access panels and performing mechanical disassembly.
steps.instruction: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is locked out and tagged out per the referenced procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: LOTO has been applied before internal access or belt tension release work begins.
steps.failure_condition: LOTO cannot be completed or is not in place before proceeding.

actions.label: Perform LOTO
actions.exact_action: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: LOTO cannot be completed before proceeding.

source_evidence.text: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
The removal steps instruct the technician to ... perform LOTO on the tipper.

steps.title: Remove the station side panels
steps.purpose: Open access to the timing belt area.
steps.instruction: Use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panels, then remove the station side panels to access the timing belt.
steps.expected_result: The side panels are removed and the timing belt area is accessible.
steps.healthy_condition: All nine self-tapping fasteners are removed and the timing belt area is exposed.
steps.failure_condition: The fasteners or panels cannot be removed as documented, or the timing belt area remains inaccessible.

actions.label: Remove side panel fasteners
actions.exact_action: Use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panels.

actions.label: Remove side panels
actions.exact_action: Remove the station side panels to access the timing belt.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Access area behind the station side panels and timing belt location.

stop_or_escalate_if: The side panel fasteners cannot be identified or removed as documented.
The timing belt area cannot be accessed after panel removal.

source_evidence.text: Use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panels, then remove the station side panels to access the timing belt.
The removal steps instruct the technician to ... remove nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panels.

steps.title: Secure both carriages with M6 screws
steps.purpose: Prevent the A-axis and counterbalance from dropping when belt tension is released.
steps.instruction: Thread M6 socket-head screws into the holes below both carriages to prevent the A-axis and counterbalance from dropping once belt tension is released.
steps.expected_result: Both carriages are secured with M6 socket-head screws below them.
steps.healthy_condition: M6 socket-head screws are installed below both carriages before belt tension is released.
steps.failure_condition: The threaded holes cannot be identified, or the M6 socket-head screws cannot be installed to secure both carriages.

actions.label: Install carriage securing screws
actions.exact_action: Thread M6 socket-head screws into the holes below both carriages to prevent the A-axis and counterbalance from dropping once belt tension is released.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Threaded holes used for the M6 socket-head screws below both carriages.
Related visual context showing M6 socket-head screws below both carriages to prevent dropping when belt tension is released.

stop_or_escalate_if: The threaded holes below both carriages cannot be identified.
The M6 socket-head screws cannot be installed to secure the carriages before belt tension is released.

source_evidence.text: Thread M6 socket-head screws into the holes below both carriages to prevent the A-axis and counterbalance from dropping once belt tension is released.
The removal steps instruct the technician to ... install M6 socket-head screws below both carriages to prevent the A-axis and counterbalance from dropping when belt tension is released.

steps.title: Remove the belt clamps
steps.purpose: Free the carriages from the timing belt before releasing belt tension and removing the belt.
steps.instruction: Remove the belt clamps by removing the six M6x20 countersunk screws from each clamp with a 5-mm hex wrench to free the carriages from the belt.
steps.expected_result: The belt clamps are removed and the carriages are freed from the belt.
steps.healthy_condition: The six M6x20 countersunk screws from each clamp are removed and the belt clamps are off.
steps.failure_condition: The belt clamps or their fasteners cannot be identified or removed as documented.

actions.label: Remove belt clamp screws
actions.exact_action: Remove the six M6x20 countersunk screws from each clamp with a 5-mm hex wrench.

actions.label: Remove belt clamps
actions.exact_action: Remove the belt clamps to free the carriages from the belt.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Belt clamps and the M6x20 countersunk screws securing them.

stop_or_escalate_if: The belt clamp fasteners cannot be identified or removed as documented.

source_evidence.text: Remove the belt clamps by removing the six M6x20 countersunk screws from each clamp with a 5-mm hex wrench to free the carriages from the belt.

steps.title: Release belt tension
steps.purpose: Loosen the idler pulley assembly so the timing belt can be removed.
steps.instruction: Release belt tension by loosening the two M6x35 socket-head screws on the bottom side of the assembly and loosening the two M8x25 socket-head screws holding the idler pulley assembly in place.
steps.expected_result: Belt tension is released and the idler pulley assembly is loosened.
steps.healthy_condition: The two M6x35 socket-head screws and two M8x25 socket-head screws are loosened as documented, allowing belt tension to be released.
steps.failure_condition: The tensioning screws or idler pulley assembly screws cannot be identified or loosened as documented.

actions.label: Loosen M6 tensioning screws
actions.exact_action: Loosen the two M6x35 socket-head screws on the bottom side of the assembly.

actions.label: Loosen idler pulley assembly screws
actions.exact_action: Loosen the two M8x25 socket-head screws holding the idler pulley assembly in place.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Idler pulley assembly and the area where the M8x25 socket-head screws hold it in place.
Timing belt removal context around the belt path and tension release area.

stop_or_escalate_if: The M6x35 socket-head screws cannot be identified or loosened as documented.
The M8x25 socket-head screws holding the idler pulley assembly cannot be identified or loosened as documented.

source_evidence.text: Release belt tension by loosening the two M6x35 socket-head screws on the bottom side of the assembly and loosening the two M8x25 socket-head screws holding the idler pulley assembly in place.

Use this artifact when searching for idler pulley assembly identification, pulley replacement, belt tension release preparation, A-axis support, counterbalance securing, or tipper maintenance visuals.

steps.title: Hold the idler assembly and remove the timing belt
steps.purpose: Complete the documented removal when only the timing belt is being replaced.
steps.instruction: If only the timing belt is being replaced, slide the idler pulley assembly up and re-tighten the two M6x35 socket-head screws to hold the idler pulley assembly in place, then remove the timing belt from the pulleys.
steps.expected_result: The idler pulley assembly is held in place and the timing belt is removed from the pulleys.
steps.healthy_condition: The timing belt is fully removed from the pulleys with the A-axis and counterbalance secured against dropping.
steps.failure_condition: The idler pulley assembly cannot be held in place as documented, or the timing belt cannot be removed from the pulleys.

actions.label: Position idler pulley assembly
actions.exact_action: Slide the idler pulley assembly up.

actions.label: Hold idler pulley assembly in place
actions.exact_action: Re-tighten the two M6x35 socket-head screws to hold the idler pulley assembly in place.

actions.label: Remove timing belt
actions.exact_action: Remove the timing belt from the pulleys.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Timing belt path on the pulleys for final belt removal.
Idler pulley assembly location while sliding it up and holding it in place.

stop_or_escalate_if: The idler pulley assembly cannot be positioned and held as documented.
The timing belt cannot be removed from the pulleys.
Additional installation, adjustment, or recovery guidance is needed beyond the documented removal scope.

source_evidence.text: If only the timing belt is being replaced, slide the idler pulley assembly up and re-tighten the two M6x35 socket-head screws to hold the idler pulley assembly in place, then remove the timing belt from the pulleys.

success_criteria: The timing belt is removed from the pulleys.
The A-axis and counterbalance remain secured against dropping during belt tension release.
The documented removal scope is completed without proceeding into unsupported installation or adjustment actions.
healthy_conditions: Required PPE, tools, and replacement part are available before work begins.
The A-axis and counterbalance are positioned between the threaded holes in the actuator base.
Two holes are visible adjacent to each rail and the carriages are positioned between the holes on the respective rails.
LOTO is applied before internal access and disassembly.
M6 socket-head screws are installed below both carriages before belt tension is released.
The belt clamps are removed and the carriages are freed from the belt.
The idler pulley assembly is loosened and then held in place as documented for belt removal.
The timing belt is removed from the pulleys.
failure_conditions: Required part, PPE, or tools are missing.
The A-axis, counterbalance, or carriages cannot be positioned as described before belt tension is released.
LOTO cannot be completed before proceeding.
The side panel fasteners, securing screws, belt clamp fasteners, or idler pulley assembly fasteners cannot be identified or removed/loosened as documented.
The threaded holes below both carriages cannot be identified or the M6 socket-head screws cannot be installed.
The timing belt cannot be removed from the pulleys.
Additional recovery, installation, or adjustment guidance is required beyond what this source section provides.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the A-axis, counterbalance, or carriages cannot be positioned as described before belt tension is released.
Escalate if the securing screws or belt clamp fasteners cannot be identified or removed as documented.
Escalate if the idler pulley assembly fasteners cannot be identified or loosened as documented.
Escalate if the task requires actions beyond the documented removal scope in this source section.
